title: Pseudolobivia fiebrigii
taxonomy: Pseudolobivia fiebrigii is classified as a species within the genus Pseudolobivia, which is a group of flowering plants in the cactus family (Cactaceae). This genus is further classified under the subfamily Cactoideae and the family Cactaceae. The species Pseudolobivia fiebrigii is native to the Bolivian and Peruvian Andes. It is a type of small to medium-sized cactus, characterized by its distinctive globular shape and short, fine spines. Pseudolobivia fiebrigii is often cultivated by cactus enthusiasts due to its unique appearance and hardy nature.
